key_insight: The authors present an advanced IMEX-DG solver capable of accurately modeling atmospheric dynamics with rotational effects.
engineering_relevance: This research improves the computational tools available for simulating complex atmospheric phenomena, which is crucial for weather forecasting and climate modeling.
method: High-order implicit-explicit discontinuous Galerkin (IMEX-DG) framework for the compressible Euler equations.
Second-order additive Runge-Kutta time integration scheme.
Utilizes matrix-free operator evaluation and adaptive non-conforming mesh capabilities.
evidence_signal: The proposed framework has been validated through convergence studies on inertia-gravity wave benchmarks and three-dimensional simulations.
watch_item: The implementation of rotational and gravitational source terms may introduce complexities that require careful treatment.
paper_title: An IMEX-DG solver with non-conforming mesh refinement for atmospheric dynamics with rotation
